title: Import Game
description: Import a game, register it, connect vocabulary pipeline, and persist state.
repo_context: Next.js App Router with React-Konva for game rendering. The project uses a component-based architecture with games registered in a central game registry.
acceptance_criteria: Game component is registered in the game registry
Vocabulary pipeline is connected and functional
Game state persists across navigation
All existing tests continue to pass

structured_rubric.label: Functional correctness
structured_rubric.description: All acceptance criteria met; feature works end-to-end

structured_rubric.label: Integration quality
structured_rubric.description: Code follows existing patterns; no hardcoded values; proper error handling

structured_rubric.label: Regression safety
structured_rubric.description: No existing tests broken; no unintended side effects

structured_rubric.label: Minimality
structured_rubric.description: No over-engineering; minimal diff; no unnecessary abstractions

structured_rubric.label: Process quality
structured_rubric.description: Clean commit history; meaningful messages; follows project conventions

title: Native Module Integration
description: Integrate a native iOS/Android module with React Native bridge. Create a native module that exposes device info (model, OS version) and implement a bridge to call it from JavaScript.
repo_context: React Native 0.76+ project with TypeScript. Uses CocoaPods for iOS dependencies and Gradle for Android. Project structure: ios/ for iOS native code, android/ for Android native code, src/ for JS/TS code.
acceptance_criteria: Native module bridge is properly configured for iOS
Native module bridge is properly configured for Android
JavaScript can call native methods to get device info
Permissions are declared in Info.plist for iOS
Permissions are declared in AndroidManifest.xml for Android
All existing tests continue to pass

structured_rubric.label: Functional correctness
structured_rubric.description: Native methods callable from JS; device info returned correctly

structured_rubric.label: Platform parity
structured_rubric.description: Works on both iOS and Android with consistent API

structured_rubric.label: Permission handling
structured_rubric.description: Permissions properly declared in platform manifests

structured_rubric.label: Regression safety
structured_rubric.description: No existing functionality broken; no gradle/cocoapod conflicts

structured_rubric.label: Minimality
structured_rubric.description: No unnecessary native code; clean module structure

title: Navigation Refactor
description: Migrate from React Navigation v5 to v6 with typed routes. Update navigation structure to use the latest React Navigation APIs with full TypeScript type safety for route params.
repo_context: React Native project using React Navigation v5 with TypeScript. Uses @react-navigation/native, @react-navigation/stack, and @react-navigation/bottom-tabs. Navigation config is in src/navigation/ directory.
acceptance_criteria: Routes are properly typed using TypeScript
Navigation state persists across app restart
Deep linking works for both iOS and Android
All existing navigation flows continue to work
Tab bar and stack navigation render correctly
All existing tests continue to pass

structured_rubric.label: Functional correctness
structured_rubric.description: All navigation flows work; no runtime errors

structured_rubric.label: Type safety
structured_rubric.description: Routes are fully typed; no 'any' types for route params

structured_rubric.label: Deep linking
structured_rubric.description: URLs open correct screens on both platforms

structured_rubric.label: Regression safety
structured_rubric.description: Existing navigation patterns preserved

structured_rubric.label: Minimality
structured_rubric.description: Minimal code changes; no unnecessary refactoring

title: Async Storage Migration
description: Migrate from legacy AsyncStorage to @react-native-async-storage/async-storage. Ensure all existing stored data is migrated correctly and the new API is used consistently throughout the codebase.
repo_context: React Native 0.72+ project. Previously used react-native AsyncStorage API. New storage should use @react-native-async-storage/async-storage which is the community recommended solution.
acceptance_criteria: All data migrations complete successfully on first launch
Existing user data is preserved after migration
Storage quota errors are handled gracefully
All storage operations use the new API consistently
Migration does not cause performance regression
All existing tests continue to pass

structured_rubric.label: Functional correctness
structured_rubric.description: Data persists correctly; no data loss

structured_rubric.label: Migration completeness
structured_rubric.description: All legacy data migrated; no orphaned storage keys

structured_rubric.label: Error handling
structured_rubric.description: Quota errors handled gracefully; fallback behavior defined

structured_rubric.label: Regression safety
structured_rubric.description: Existing features unaffected by migration

structured_rubric.label: Minimality
structured_rubric.description: Clean migration path; no duplicated storage logic
